Commercial Mar 26, 2026

8 Hours In A Boeing 737 MAX? Inside United Airlines' New Longest Nonstop Flights

Half of the ten longest routes started in 2025 or will do so in 2026. See them all here!

United's expansion of ultra-long 737 MAX routes signals renewed confidence in the aircraft's profitability and market acceptance post-grounding, while tapping demand for convenient nonstop alternatives to connecting itineraries.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

I'm A Flight Attendant, And These Are The Things I Wish Every Passenger Knew Before Boarding

Practical advice for passengers that makes flights easier.

Flight crew insights on passenger behavior reveal operational friction points that airlines could address through pre-flight communication to improve safety and efficiency.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

New Route & 9 More Boosted: JetBlue Keeps Turning Up The Heat In Fort Lauderdale

JetBlue adds Cleveland and boosts nine routes as Fort Lauderdale growth accelerates again.

JetBlue's strategic Fort Lauderdale expansion signals confidence in Florida's leisure travel resilience and competitive pressure on rivals' hub operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Aircraft Set To Replace Delta Air Lines' Iconic Boeing 717

Delta's 717 fleet is soon to be retired, and the airline will be replacing the type's operations with the Airbus A220 family.

Delta's shift from 717s to A220s represents a strategic modernization that reduces fuel consumption by 25% while expanding payload capacity, directly improving unit economics on regional routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The 10 Most Popular Transpacific Routes Operated By The Boeing 787 Dreamliner In 2026

The 787 and Transpacific routes really go hand-in-hand. The aircraft provides the capacity and efficiency to make these routes work.

The 787's fuel efficiency and 242-330 seat capacity enable previously marginal transpacific routes to achieve profitability, reshaping long-haul network planning for carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Effective SMS depends on psychological safety that encourages frontline reporting, which requires leadership commitment to treating safety data as improvement opportunities rather than punitive records.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April's celestial events, including peak meteor activity and a bright comet appearance, provide natural navigation reference points that support traditional piloting skills.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope completion marks a significant advancement in space-based observational capabilities following its final assembly and pre-launch testing phases.

See NASA →

The Simple Flying Podcast Episode 284: Air Canada Crash Latest, United Airlines Introduces Couches In Economy Class

Join Tom & Channing for the latest aviation hot topics.

Air Canada's latest incident and United's premium economy seating strategy signal shifting industry priorities between safety oversight and cabin revenue optimization.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion and prelaunch testing phase signals NASA's readiness for a major astrophysics mission that will expand space observation capabilities beyond current platforms.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS represents a fundamental shift from post-accident investigation to continuous risk identification, fundamentally changing how operators prevent incidents rather than just analyze them.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

Artemis II's international crew composition signals NASA's strategy to strengthen partnerships while advancing lunar exploration capabilities beyond U.S. borders.

See NASA →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Booking Last-Minute Business Class Award Tickets: Everything You Need To Know

Last-minute business class award tickets can offer significant savings relative to purchasing the fares in cash, although this is not guaranteed.

Award ticket availability for business class peaks within 24-48 hours of departure, creating arbitrage opportunities for frequent flyers who monitor elite airline partnerships strategically.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding as it looks to expand its…...

AI-driven pilot training platforms are securing significant venture capital as airlines seek cost-effective alternatives to traditional flight instruction methods.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Thermal management specialists like Rose are essential to enabling time-sensitive biological research on ISS by ensuring payload viability during complex launch and docking sequences.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's parallel runways requires precise adherence to specific procedures that differ from standard departures, making pilot briefing and compliance critical to maintaining safe separation.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Airbus Tests Multi-Aircraft Aerial Firefighting System

Trial linked aircraft, drones and ground crews for enhanced targeting and coordination.

Networked aerial firefighting systems combining manned aircraft, drones, and ground coordination can significantly improve targeting precision and response efficiency in wildfire suppression operations.

Russian aviation blogger Pavel Koshkin was killed when the Alto NG aircraft he was flying crashed near Kolomna,…...

Alpine routing near major peaks requires precise navigation planning and weather monitoring to maintain safe separation from terrain in mountainous regions.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

“Please Do Something”: Pilots Have Complained About LaGuardia Airport’s Safety For Years

Pilots at LaGuardia Airport have reported multiple near-miss situations due to unclear instructions from air traffic control.

Systemic ATC communication failures at LaGuardia create liability exposure and suggest similar unresolved safety issues likely exist at other major congested hubs.

See Simple Flying →

▶
Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist because pilots systematically underestimate detection and reaction time at operational speeds, making wire avoidance training and route planning critical rather than optional safety measures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

House Committee Advances ALERT Act

Measure includes collision-mitigation requirements, changes for military aircraft operations and limits on some ADS-B data use.

Military aircraft will face new operational restrictions while civilian operators gain collision-mitigation protections, marking a shift toward harmonized airspace safety standards across all user classes.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

This Airline Has The World’s Most Comfortable Premium Economy Seat In 2026

The Dubai-based carrier's premium economy product is relatively new.

Emirates' competitive differentiation in premium economy reflects carriers' strategic focus on capturing the high-yield segment between economy and business class.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find

For the second consecutive year, winter sea ice in the Arctic reached a level that matches the lowest peak observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979. On March 15, Arctic sea ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles (14.29 million square kilometers), very close to the 2025 peak of 5.53...

Declining Arctic sea ice reduces reflectivity and increases atmospheric heat absorption, potentially affecting polar jet streams that influence flight routing efficiency and weather prediction accuracy for northern routes.

See NASA →

Military Mar 26, 2026

5 Structural Advantages Of The A-10 Warthog For Close Air Support Missions

This aircraft offers some impressive offensive and defensive capabilities.

The A-10's redundant hydraulic systems and armor distribution enable it to absorb battle damage that would ground other military aircraft, directly extending operational availability in contested environments.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers a multiplier effect for sustainability gains that single-technology solutions cannot achieve alone.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy...

IXPE's polarimetry data on RCW 86 reveals supernova physics invisible to traditional telescopes, advancing understanding of extreme cosmic events.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Colorado’s Rocky Mountain Metro Airport Launches Unleaded Avgas

UL94 sales begin with state-backed price support and STC reimbursement.

State-level price subsidies and STC reimbursement programs are emerging as practical adoption tools for unleaded fuel transition, potentially influencing how other regions accelerate the avgas phase-out.

See AVweb →

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic supersonic fighter production demonstrates how technology transfer partnerships enable emerging economies to build advanced military aviation capabilities independently.

Kenya Airways returned to financial losses in 2025, the airline reported on March 24, 2026. The Kenyan flag…...

Extended aircraft groundings demonstrate how supply chain disruptions can rapidly erode airline profitability even for carriers with otherwise stable operations.

AMA Advocacy: H.R. 6460 passes U.S. House of Representatives

On Tuesday, March 24, H.R. 6460, a bill championed by AMA that will simplify altitude...

H.R. 6460's passage streamlines regulatory compliance for recreational drone operators by raising operational altitude limits, reducing bureaucratic barriers that have hindered hobby aviation participation and innovation.

Curiosity's extended boxwork terrain investigation demonstrates how rovers optimize science output through coordinated observation sequences, informing strategies for future Mars missions.

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's April 1 target date for Artemis II represents a critical validation of human deep-space mission capabilities with live coverage enhancing public engagement in lunar exploration.
